identity.middle_name: none (omit the line entirely on any form; do not invent one)

location.eastern_time_zone: No. Based in Pacific Time, but can work Eastern Time hours if needed.
location.willing_onsite_first_week: Yes, if travel and logistics allow.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

employment.actively_interviewing: Yes. In process with a few companies at early to mid stages.
employment.pending_offers: In active processes but no signed offer yet.

work_authorization.status: Permanent Resident / U.S. Green Card holder
work_authorization.canonical_wording: I am authorized to work in the United States through a Green Card and do not require sponsorship.
work_authorization.c2c: Open to W2; also open to C2C, whichever the company prefers.
work_authorization.w2_1099: Yes to W2; also open to 1099 if needed.
work_authorization.security_clearance: None currently, but able to obtain one.
work_authorization.regulated_environments: Yes. Worked in regulated enterprise environments (Domino's systems) with strict controls around payments, logging, data handling, and operational reliability.

positioning.core_line: Senior backend-leaning software engineer and technical lead who builds production systems, data pipelines, internal tools, AI workflows, and cross-functional teams that actually ship.
positioning.primary_lane: Senior Backend Engineer, Senior Python Engineer, AI Backend Engineer, Platform / Data Infrastructure Engineer, Technical Lead, Full Stack Engineer.
positioning.secondary_lane: Full-Stack Engineer only when backend is still the core. Engineering Manager only when the role values technical depth and hands-on leadership.
positioning.apps_deployed: Close to a hundred over the span of his career.
positioning.stronger_in: Backend (clearly), with data and AI as primary lanes; knowledgeable but not specialist in frontend.

years_of_experience._note: Numbers to use when a form or recruiter asks 'years of X'. Explain projects, not just time, in conversation.

salary_form_fill._note: ONLY for required form fields. NEVER state a number in a conversation or email reply. In a conversation, ask the role's budget first.
